assertion_results.text: Data extraction correctly identifies CVE-2026-31812, library quinn-proto, affected range < 0.11.14, and fixed version 0.11.14 from the Vulnerability issue (Step 1)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md lines 49-56: CVE ID = CVE-2026-31812, Vulnerable library = quinn-proto, Affected version range = < 0.11.14, Fixed version = 0.11.14

assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as Cargo based on the library name and component context (Step 1 Ecosystem detection)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md line 75: 'Ecosystem: **Cargo**' with explanation 'Library: quinn-proto (a Rust crate)' and verification against Ecosystem Mappings table

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the security-matrix.md supportability matrix — no versions are skipped (Important Rule 4)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md lines 54-60 show all 7 versions: 2.1.0, 2.1.1, 2.2.0, 2.2.1, 2.2.2, 2.2.3, 2.2.4 across both streams

assertion_results.text: Version impact analysis uses pinned commit tags from the supportability matrix for each version, not branch HEAD (Important Rule 13)
Version impact table identifies retag versions and carries forward the affected status from the original version (Important Rule 5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md line 44: '2.2.2 | v0.4.9 | -- | YES | retag of 2.2.1 (same as 2.2.1: 0.11.12)' — correctly identified as retag and carries forward YES status

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction proposes replacing PSIRT-assigned versions with lock-file-verified versions, showing the diff (Step 3.2)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md lines 56-57: 'Current Affects Versions (PSIRT-assigned): [RHTPA 2.0.0]' and 'Proposed Affects Versions (lock-file-verified): [RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2]'

assertion_results.text: Affects Versions correction references version names from the supportability matrix rather than hardcoded Jira version IDs (Important Rule 6)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md line 76: '(Jira version IDs discovered dynamically from Step 3.1 -- not hardcoded.)' and uses version names like RHTPA 2.2.0, RHTPA 2.2.1, RHTPA 2.2.2 with placeholder IDs '<RHTPA-2.2.0-jira-id>'

assertion_results.text: Remediation tasks include Repository, Target Branch, acceptance criteria, and dependency linkage between upstream and downstream tasks (Important Rule 9)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md Task 1 has Repository (rhtpa-backend), Target Branch (release/0.4.z), Acceptance Criteria section. Task 2 has Repository (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), Target Branch (main), Acceptance Criteria. Jira Linkage section at lines 173-199 shows Depend links to TC-8001 and Blocks link between upstream and downstream

assertion_results.text: For Cargo ecosystem, remediation creates two tasks: upstream backport task and downstream propagation subtask with Blocks dependency (Important Rule 8)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md lines 35-39: 'two tasks: 1. Upstream backport task -- fix in the source repo (rhtpa-backend) 2. Downstream propagation subtask -- update the reference in the Konflux release repo. The downstream subtask is blocked by the upstream task.' Blocks link at line 188

assertion_results.text: The analysis presents triage recommendations as proposed actions — framing Affects Versions changes, label additions, and status transitions as proposals rather than executed mutations (Guardrails)
assertion_results.evidence: affects-versions.md line 64: 'Proposed action (requires engineer confirmation)'. remediation.md line 169: 'the following Jira mutations are proposed (each requires engineer confirmation)'. All actions consistently framed as 'Proposed'
